paper_title: Analysis of Pleistocene Mammalian Remains and Associated Sediments

methods.method_name: Pleistocene Mammalian Remains Collection and Sediment Sampling
methods.description: This method describes the process of selecting and collecting blackened Pleistocene mammalian remains from northeastern Russia, primarily from river valleys, lakes, and coasts where they accumulate due to permafrost thawing. It also includes the specific sampling of sediment infilling from the brain cases of selected skulls for subsequent analysis.

steps.description: Select blackened Pleistocene mammalian remains that were collected by local residents from northeastern Russia (Fig. 1) and are housed in the National Alliance of Shidlovskiy 'Ice Age' collection, Moscow.
steps.searchable_summary: Select specific mammalian fossil specimens from a collection based on origin and condition.
inputs: Collection of Pleistocene mammalian remains (National Alliance of Shidlovskiy 'Ice Age' collection)
outputs: Selected mammalian fossil specimens (e.g., skulls, mandible, horns)

steps.description: Note specific specimens (i) skulls of the cave lion (Panthera spelaea) F-4299, ancient bison (Bison priscus) F-4236, and woolly rhinoceros (Coelodonta antiquitatis) F-61 from the middle-lower reaches of the Indigirka River, upstream of Chokurdakh township; (ii) skull, mandible, nasal, and frontal horns of a single individual of woolly rhinoceros, F-506-F-509 from the channel of the Malaya Kuropatoch'ya River; (iii) nasal horn of woolly rhinoceros F-1990 from the vicinity of Chersky township, Lower Kolyma River.
steps.searchable_summary: Document specific selected specimens and their detailed collection locations.
outputs: List of specific specimens with associated metadata (ID, species, type of remain, collection location, coordinates)

steps.description: From the cerebral cavity of the skulls of F-4299, F-506 and F-61, sediment infilling samples were taken to analyse the granulometric composition and perform palaeobotanical studies.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect sediment samples from specific locations within selected fossil specimens.
outputs: Sediment infilling samples from skull cavities

methods.method_name: Sediment Grain-size Analysis by Laser Diffractometry
methods.description: This method details the procedure for determining the granulometric composition of sediment samples, specifically focusing on silicate minerals. It involves preliminary chemical treatment to remove organic matter and carbonates, followed by dispersion and analysis using laser diffractometry.

steps.description: Remove organic matter and carbonates during the preliminary preparation.
steps.searchable_summary: Chemically remove organic matter and carbonates from sediment samples.
outputs: Sediment samples with organic matter and carbonates removed

steps.description: Preparation of samples included sequential treatment with a 10% solution of hydrochloric acid (1 h) and a 30% solution of hydrogen peroxide heated in a water bath to 90°C (over 3 h, before bleaching the material).
steps.searchable_summary: Perform sequential chemical treatments: HCl (10%, 1h) then H2O2 (30%, 90°C, >3h).
inputs: Sediment samples after initial removal step

steps.description: After treatment with reagents, the material was transferred with a pipette to the liquid cell of the dispersion unit of the Hydro EV analyser.
steps.searchable_summary: Transfer treated sediment material to the dispersion unit liquid cell.
outputs: Sediment material in dispersion unit liquid cell

steps.description: In the cell, the material was exposed for 100 s to ultrasound with a capacity of 70% and was intensively mixed with a special turntable at a speed of 2400 rpm.
steps.searchable_summary: Disperse sediment material using ultrasound (100s, 70%) and intensive mixing (2400 rpm).
inputs: Sediment material in dispersion unit liquid cell

steps.description: Grain-size analysis of infilling sediments from brain cases of skulls F-4299, F-506, and F-61 was performed by laser diffractometry on a Malvern Mastersizer 3000 granulometer in the Institute of Geography, Russian Academy of Sciences (RAS), Moscow.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform laser diffractometry on dispersed sediment samples.

steps.description: Particle sizes were calculated based on the Mi model with standard values (Ozer et al. 2010) for the refractive index and absorption (n = 1.45; k = 0.1).
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate particle size distribution from raw diffractometry data using the Mi model with specified parameters.

methods.description: This method describes the isolation and microscopic analysis of various microfossils (phytoliths, diatoms, sponge spicules, pollen) present in the sediment infilling of skull F-506. It involves a specific isolation procedure followed by examination using both light microscopy and scanning electron microscopy (SEM).

steps.description: Other microfossils (phytoliths, diatoms, sponge spicules) from the infilling sediment in the F-506 woolly rhinoceros skull were isolated in accordance with the procedure described in Kirillova et al. (2020).
steps.searchable_summary: Isolate microfossils from sediment sample using a referenced procedure.

steps.description: The resulting material in glycerine was examined under a Carl Zeiss Axiostar Plus microscope, 100-4009, at pH 2.
steps.searchable_summary: Prepare isolated microfossil material in glycerine and examine using light microscopy.

steps.description: Measurement of microfossils from the dried sample was carried out under a Vega 3 Tescan scanning electron microscope (SEM) in high and variable vacuum mode accompanied by BSE and SE analysers in the Institute of Physicochemical and Biological Problems of Soil Science, Pushchino.
steps.searchable_summary: Measure and image microfossils from a dried sample using Scanning Electron Microscopy (SEM).
outputs: SEM images and measurements of microfossils

steps.description: Diatoms. -Frustules and separate valves of diatoms of at least 10 species belonging to the Pleistocene and extant freshwater unicellulars were found in specimen F-506. ... The species are illustrated by SEM images (Fig. S1). Sponges. -Multiple fragmented sponges are represented including single megascleres of oxea type. ... Phytoliths. -A large number of various phytoliths were discovered in specimen F-506 (Fig. S2). ... Pollen. -In the infilling sediment of the skull F-506, arboreal (tree and shrub) pollen (AP) content reaches 30% of the total terrestrial pollen and spore sum (Σ). ... In the infilling sediment from the skull F-61, AP makes up 45% of Σ, NAP: 48%, and spores: 6% of the sum ...
steps.searchable_summary: Identify, classify, and quantify different types of microfossils (diatoms, sponges, phytoliths, pollen, spores) found in the samples.

methods.method_name: Infrared Spectral Study of Bone and Horn
methods.description: This method involves analyzing the chemical composition of bone and horn samples using Infrared (IR) absorption spectroscopy. It aims to identify characteristic absorption bands corresponding to mineral components (orthophosphate, carbonate) and organic components (protein, lipids) to assess the preservation state and composition of the samples.

steps.description: Obtain control sample (fresh human bone; Fig. 5).
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire a fresh human bone sample as a control.

steps.description: Obtain study samples from the mandible and horn of the woolly rhinoceros (F-507 and F-509).
steps.searchable_summary: Acquire woolly rhinoceros bone (mandible) and horn samples.

steps.description: Perform IR absorption spectroscopy on the control sample and the studied samples.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect Infrared (IR) absorption spectra from bone and horn samples.

steps.description: Analyze the IR absorption spectra to identify characteristic absorption bands associated with splitting in the PO4 3− region (500-650 cm−1, 900-1150 cm−1), anion absorption bands CO3 2− (880, 1430 and 1460 cm−1), absorption bands related to vibrations in the protein region (amide III 1240 cm−1, amide II 1540±10 cm−1, amide I 1660±20 cm−1) including methyl CH2 and CH3 groups (2850±30 cm−1, 2925-2970 cm−1), and a lipid absorption band at 1740±15 cm−1.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and assign specific peaks/bands in IR spectra to chemical functional groups.
outputs: Identified IR peaks/bands and their assignments

steps.description: Interpret identified peaks/bands as indicative of bone mineral (orthophosphate crystal lattice, carbonate) and organic components (amide intensity I, II, III, methyl groups, lipids).
steps.searchable_summary: Interpret identified IR peaks/bands in terms of chemical composition and bone structure.
inputs: Identified IR peaks/bands and their assignments
outputs: Interpretation of sample chemical composition based on IR spectra

steps.description: Compare the IR absorption spectra of the studied samples from the mandible and horn of the woolly rhinoceros (F-507 and F-509) to the control sample and other well-preserved mammal specimens.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare IR spectra of study samples to control and reference spectra.
